bead.title: bench: runner embeds full profile snapshot in cells
bead.description:
Modify the cell-write boundary so every report.json embeds the full resolved profile snapshot, plus framework, dataset, and a new cell_id.

The current cell write at cmd/bench/matrix.go (around the area that stamps profile_id + profile_snapshot, near line 751) writes profile_id only. New behavior: serialize the resolved Profile struct into the report.json as an embedded 'profile' field.

Cell record additions:
- profile: <full resolved Profile struct, including all metadata, sampling, limits, pricing, harness, surface, concurrency_group, versioning>
- framework: string (from bench-set, e.g. 'terminal-bench')
- dataset: string (from bench-set, e.g. 'terminal-bench-2-1')
- cell_id: string (ISO timestamp + short random, e.g. '20260516T103045Z-a4c1')

cell_id generation:
  time.Now().UTC().Format("20060102T150405Z") + "-" + shortRandom(4)
where shortRandom returns a 4-char hex from crypto/rand.

The cell directory layout changes: new cells write to cells/<framework>-<dataset>/<task>/<cell-id>/report.json (no profile_id in path, no rep-NNN). Backfill (PR 2) brings old cells into this layout.

profile_id and profile_snapshot string fields stay on the cell record for backward-compatibility through PR 3 (analytics switch); PR 4 deletes them.

In-scope files:
- cmd/bench/matrix.go (cell write boundary)
- cmd/bench/matrix_test.go (extend coverage)
- internal/benchmark/profile/profile.go (if a helper for resolved-snapshot serialization is needed)

Out-of-scope:
- New shell driver (PR 1d)
- Backfill of existing cells (PR 2)
- Analytics switch (PR 3)
- Deletion of lane scaffolding (PR 4)

Spec: docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-05-15-benchmark-runner-simplification.md section 'PR 1c'
bead.acceptance:
1. 'go test ./cmd/bench/...' passes.
2. Smoke run: 'fiz-bench sweep --recipe canary --lanes fiz-noop' (or equivalent) produces cells whose report.json contains a 'profile' object with non-empty 'provider', 'sampling', 'limits', 'metadata', and 'versioning' subfields.
3. 'jq ".profile.id, .framework, .dataset, .cell_id" benchmark-results/.../report.json' returns four non-null values for a freshly-written cell.
4. Old cells (pre-backfill) remain loadable by existing analytics code.
